basics.name: Ramazan Bora
basics.summary: Economics PhD candidate in empirical economics and political economy focused on granular open data across space, time, and behavior.

work.company: Queensland Centre for Population Research (School of the Environment, UQ)
work.summary: Developed reproducible demographic estimation pipelines and methods for small-area Indigenous population estimation.
Refactored and documented core R workflows and built reproducible comparative data pipelines.

work.company: Real Estate and Transport Project (Prof. Alicia Rambaldi)
work.summary: Integrated property transaction data with GIS and transport networks for econometric analysis.
Built social media text analytics and sentiment workflows.
Developed data tools and supported theory research in economics.
Applied pattern recognition and signal-processing methods.

education.institution: Penn State University and Ibn Haldun University

education.area: BSc in Electrical and Electronics Engineering

publications.name: Birthplace Favoritism Revisited: Replication, Modern Evidence, and Crisis Dynamics
publications.publisher: UQ School of Economics Discussion Paper Series
publications.summary: Replication and extension study using open-source data and VIIRS night lights with crisis dynamics analysis.

publications.name: A High-Resolution View of the Hinterland: Local Government Centralization and Rural Development in Turkey
publications.summary: Border-based DiD analysis of Turkey's 2012 metropolitan reform with newly reconstructed village-level data.

publications.name: Measuring Cognition at Scale: The Association Between Ramadan Exposure and Chess Performance
publications.summary: Move-level analysis of 16 million chess observations using Stockfish benchmarking and DiD identification.
